**Liverpool secured a dramatic 2-1 win over Everton in the Merseyside derby, with Virgil van Dijk scoring the winner in the 100th minute.**

## Context / Background
The match was played at Everton's new home, Hill Dickinson Stadium, and was the first Merseyside derby to be held at the venue. Liverpool came into the match looking to boost their Champions League hopes, while Everton were seeking to cause an upset and gain valuable points in their fight against relegation.

## Tactical Key Points / Stats Table
| Team | Possession | Shots | Corners |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Liverpool | 55% | 15 | 6 |
| Everton | 45% | 10 | 4 |

## Key Quote
> 'We showed great mentality and character to come back and win the game,' said Liverpool manager Arne Slot.

## What's Next?
Liverpool will look to build on this win as they continue their push for a top-four finish in the Premier League. Their next match is against a tough opponent, and they will need to be at their best to secure all three points.
